output:
# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory backend for a collaborative team workspace.  It
provides user management, project and task tracking, threaded messaging with
attachments, a performance dashboard (completion stats and peer ratings) and
exportable reports (CSV / PDF).

The public surface intentionally mirrors the test-suite contract:

* Enums:        ``TaskStatus``, ``UserRole``, ``ReportFormat``, ``ReportType``
* Exceptions:   ``TeamCollaborationError`` and its specialisations
* Entities:     ``Attachment``, ``User``, ``Project``, ``Task``, ``Message``,
                ``Rating``
* Facade:       ``TeamCollaborationManager``

Standard library only.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import json
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states a task can occupy."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorisation level for a registered user."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export serialisations."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Discriminators for the report dictionaries the manager produces."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint is violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with existing state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity data classes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered participant."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A unit of work owned by a user and bounded by a date range."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A single actionable item belonging to a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A threaded message posted against a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """Peer feedback given by one user to another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal collection wrapper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Collection:
    """A thin keyed store that shares a single lock with its siblings.

    Every collection exposes the *same* lock object so that compound,
    cross-collection operations can be performed atomically through the
    manager's shared lock.
    """

    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        self._lock = lock
        self._items: Dict[int, object] = {}


def _clone(obj):
    """Return a deep, isolated copy so callers cannot mutate stored state."""

    return copy.deepcopy(obj)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Coordinates all team-collaboration operations.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        A zero-argument callable returning the "current" :class:`datetime`.
        Injectable for deterministic testing; defaults to ``datetime.now``.
    """

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        # A single re-entrant lock guards every collection.  Re-entrancy lets
        # high-level methods call helper methods that also acquire the lock.
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        self.users = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Collection(self._lock)
        self.reports = _Collection(self._lock)

    # -- id allocation ----------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        """Allocate the next globally-unique id.  Caller must hold the lock."""

        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    # -- internal lookups (caller holds the lock) -------------------------
    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task  # type: ignore[return-value]

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message  # type: ignore[return-value]

    # -- validation helpers ----------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _clean_username(username: str) -> str:
        if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
            raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
        return username.strip()

    @staticmethod
    def _clean_email(email: str) -> str:
        if not isinstance(email, str):
            raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
        cleaned = email.strip()
        if not cleaned or "@" not in cleaned:
            raise ValidationError("email must be non-empty and contain '@'")
        return cleaned

    # ====================================================================
    # User management
    # ====================================================================
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        """Create and store a new user."""

        with self._lock:
            uname = self._clean_username(username)
            mail = self._clean_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            for existing in self.users._items.values():
                if existing.username == uname:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
                if existing.email == mail:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=uname,
                email=mail,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users._items[user.id] = user
            return _clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                _clone(u)
                for u in sorted(self.users._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Optional[str] = None,
        email: Optional[str] = None,
        role: Optional[UserRole] = None,
    ) -> User:
        """Update mutable fields of a user, validating before applying."""

        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            new_username = user.username
            new_email = user.email
            new_role = user.role

            if username is not None:
                new_username = self._clean_username(username)
                for existing in self.users._items.values():
                    if existing.id != user_id and existing.username == new_username:
                        raise DuplicateError("username already in use")

            if email is not None:
                new_email = self._clean_email(email)
                for existing in self.users._items.values():
                    if existing.id != user_id and existing.email == new_email:
                        raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            if role is not None:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
                new_role = role

            user.username = new_username
            user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            return _clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        """Remove a user, refusing if they own projects or hold open tasks."""

        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project and cannot be deleted")

            for task in self.tasks._items.values():
                if task.assignee_id == user_id and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "user has open tasks and cannot be deleted"
                    )

            del self.users._items[user_id]

    # ====================================================================
    # Project management
    # ====================================================================
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        """Create a project owned by an existing user."""

        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("project name must be non-empty")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")

            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=name.strip(),
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
            )
            self.projects._items[project.id] = project
            return _clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            return [
                _clone(p)
                for p in sorted(self.projects._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Project:
        """Update mutable project fields, keeping the date range coherent."""

        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            if name is not None:
                if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("project name must be non-empty")
                new_name = name.strip()

            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            return _clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a project and cascade to its tasks and messages."""

        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            task_ids = {
                tid
                for tid, task in self.tasks._items.items()
                if task.project_id == project_id
            }

            # Remove messages bound to the project or any of its tasks.
            doomed_messages = [
                mid
                for mid, msg in self.messages._items.items()
                if msg.project_id == project_id or msg.task_id in task_ids
            ]
            for mid in doomed_messages:
                del self.messages._items[mid]

            for tid in task_ids:
                del self.tasks._items[tid]

            del self.projects._items[project_id]

    # ====================================================================
    # Task management
    # ====================================================================
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Create a task within a project, optionally assigned with a deadline."""

        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("task title must be non-empty")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")

            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title.strip(),
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task.id] = task
            return _clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            return [
                _clone(t)
                for t in sorted(self.tasks._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            return [
                _clone(t)
                for t in sorted(self.tasks._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
                if t.assignee_id == assignee_id
            ]

    def _set_assignee(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> Task:
        """Shared assignment routine.  Caller must hold the lock."""

        task = self._require_task(task_id)
        if assignee_id is not None:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
        task.assignee_id = assignee_id
        return task

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._set_assignee(task_id, assignee_id))

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._set_assignee(task_id, new_assignee_id))

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return _clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return _clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        """Transition a task's status, maintaining started/completed stamps."""

        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            now = self._now()

            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now

            task.status = status
            return _clone(task)

    # ====================================================================
    # Messaging
    # ====================================================================
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[Sequence[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        """Post a message against exactly one of a project or a task."""

        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            attachment_objs = self._build_attachments(author_id, attachments)

            body_text = body if isinstance(body, str) else ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not attachment_objs:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "message must have a body or at least one attachment"
                )

            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body_text,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=attachment_objs,
                created_at=self._now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message.id] = message
            return _clone(message)

    def _build_attachments(
        self,
        uploader_id: int,
        attachments: Optional[Sequence[Tuple[str, bytes]]],
    ) -> List[Attachment]:
        """Materialise (filename, data) pairs into Attachment objects."""

        result: List[Attachment] = []
        if not attachments:
            return result
        now = self._now()
        for filename, data in attachments:
            payload = data if data is not None else b""
            result.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(payload),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
            )
        return result

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            body_text = new_body if isinstance(new_body, str) else ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("edited message body must be non-empty")
            message.body = body_text
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return _clone(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may attach files")
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("filename must be non-empty")
            payload = data if data is not None else b""
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(payload),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._now(),
                )
            )
            return _clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            return [
                _clone(m)
                for m in sorted(self.messages._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
                if m.project_id == project_id
            ]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            return [
                _clone(m)
                for m in sorted(self.messages._items.values(), key=lambda x: x.id)
                if m.task_id == task_id
            ]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            del self.messages._items[message_id]

    # ====================================================================
    # Performance dashboard
    # ====================================================================
    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, assignee_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [
            t for t in self.tasks._items.values() if t.assignee_id == assignee_id
        ]

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            return self._average_completion_time(self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id))

    @staticmethod
    def _average_completion_time(tasks: List[Task]) -> Optional[float]:
        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.started_at is not None
            and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        if not durations:
            return None
        return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        """Record peer feedback from one user about another."""

        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance._items[rating.id] = rating
            return _clone(rating)

    def _ratings_for(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        return [
            r for r in self.performance._items.values() if r.ratee_id == user_id
        ]

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = sorted(self._ratings_for(user_id), key=lambda r: r.id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [_clone(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ====================================================================
    # Reports
    # ====================================================================
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                t for t in self.tasks._items.values() if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            now = self._now()

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            completed = 0

            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.deadline < now
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id, {"total": 0, "completed": 0}
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1

            total = len(tasks)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project.id,
                "project_name": project.name,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)

            # Map each in-scope user to the tasks counted toward their stats.
            scoped: Dict[int, List[Task]] = {}
            for task in self.tasks._items.values():
                if project_id is not None and task.project_id != project_id:
                    continue
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                scoped.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            rows = []
            for user_id in sorted(scoped):
                tasks = scoped[user_id]
                total = len(tasks)
                completed = sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                )
                ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
                rating_count = len(ratings)
                rating_average = (
                    sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                    if rating_count
                    else 0.0
                )
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": user_id,
                        "total_tasks": total,
                        "completed_tasks": completed,
                        "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                        "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(
                            tasks
                        ),
                        "rating_count": rating_count,
                        "rating_average": rating_average,
                    }
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "scope_project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(
                self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            ratings = self._ratings_for(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )

            task_rows = [
                {
                    "task_id": t.id,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "deadline": t.deadline.isoformat() if t.deadline else "",
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # -- export -----------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report: dict, fmt: ReportFormat):
        """Serialise a report dictionary to CSV text or PDF bytes."""

        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a dictionary")

        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._export_csv(report)
        return self._export_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _export_csv(report: dict) -> str:
        out = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(out, lineterminator="\n")

        rows = report.get("rows")
        if report.get("report_type") == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value or (
            isinstance(rows, list)
            and rows
            and all(isinstance(r, dict) for r in rows)
        ):
            header = [
                "user_id",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_count",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            writer.writerow(header)
            for row in rows or []:
                writer.writerow([row.get(col, "") for col in header])
            return out.getvalue()

        # Generic field/value serialisation for scalar-oriented reports.
        writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
        for key, value in report.items():
            if isinstance(value, (list, dict)):
                writer.writerow([key, json.dumps(value, default=str, sort_keys=True)])
            else:
                writer.writerow([key, value])
        return out.getvalue()

    @staticmethod
    def _export_pdf(report: dict) -> bytes:
        # Build a small, structurally-valid single-page PDF.  Content is the
        # report rendered as plain text lines.
        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if isinstance(value, (list, dict)):
                value = json.dumps(value, default=str, sort_keys=True)
            lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))

        def _sanitize(text: str) -> str:
            ascii_text = str(text).encode("ascii", "replace").decode("ascii")
            ascii_text = ascii_text.replace("\\", "/").replace("(", "[").replace(
                ")", "]"
            )
            return ascii_text[:160]

        content_ops = ["BT", "/F1 11 Tf", "72 760 Td", "13 TL"]
        for line in lines:
            content_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(_sanitize(line)))
            content_ops.append("T*")
        content_ops.append("ET")
        stream = "\n".join(content_ops)

        objects = [
            "<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            "<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                "<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                "/Contents 4 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>"
            ),
            "<< /Length {0} >>\nstream\n{1}\nendstream".format(len(stream), stream),
            "<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = "%PDF-1.4\n"
        offsets = []
        for index, obj in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf.encode("latin-1")))
            pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n{1}\nendobj\n".format(index, obj)

        xref_pos = len(pdf.encode("latin-1"))
        count = len(objects) + 1
        pdf += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(count)
        pdf += "0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset)
        pdf += "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\n".format(count)
        pdf += "startxref\n{0}\n%%EOF".format(xref_pos)
        return pdf.encode("latin-1")
